pending.body: `src/contexts/milestone_record/service.rs:417-474` seals the lineage row before updating the snapshot or journal, but `src/adapters/fs.rs:2545-2556` rejects any later finalize on an already-terminal entry. If `write_snapshot` or `append_event` fails after the row update, retrying `record_bead_completion`/`update_task_run` can no longer repair the snapshot/journal drift, so a transient I/O failure leaves milestone state permanently inconsistent unless `task-runs.ndjson` is edited manually. Make completion idempotent for the same terminal payload, or defer sealing the lineage row until the rest of the milestone state is durable.

`src/contexts/milestone_record/service.rs:305-308` moves a started bead into `MilestoneStatus::Active`, but `src/contexts/milestone_record/service.rs:448-456` clears `active_bead` on completion without changing `status` or re-validating. `src/contexts/milestone_record/model.rs:172-176` explicitly defines `status == Active && active_bead == None` as inconsistent, so every normal completion now persists a snapshot that violates the model invariant. Completion needs to transition out of `Active` (or otherwise preserve the invariant) before writing the snapshot.

run_snapshot.status_summary: running: final review restart round 4 -> Plan and Implement
